items.prompt: To divide one complex number by another, z1/z2 with z2 not 0, the standard method is to:
items.answer: multiply numerator and denominator by the conjugate of the denominator

expected.correct: multiply numerator and denominator by the conjugate of the denominator
options: multiply numerator and denominator by the conjugate of the denominator
divide the real parts and the imaginary parts separately
multiply numerator and denominator by i
take the reciprocal of each part

items.srcLabel: Authored practice (Opus) - maths-ch04 Sec 4.3.4 Division of two complex numbers

items.prompt: Compute the modulus |2 - 5i| = sqrt(a^2 + b^2). Give the decimal value to two places.

items.prompt: Using the cube identity, expand (5 - 3i)^3 and give its real part.
items.srcLabel: Authored practice (Opus) - maths-ch04 Sec 4.3.7 Identities, Example 3 ((5 - 3i)^3 = -10 - 198i)

items.prompt: Evaluate sqrt(-2) x sqrt(-3) correctly by first writing each root in i-form. Give the decimal value to two places.
items.srcLabel: Authored practice (Opus) - maths-ch04 Sec 4.3.6 sqrt(a)sqrt(b) not= sqrt(ab) for negatives

items.prompt: Explain geometrically, in the Argand plane, where the conjugate z-bar = x - iy of a complex number z = x + iy lies relative to z, and why z and z-bar have the same modulus.

expected.rubric: z = x + iy is the point P(x, y); its conjugate z-bar = x - iy is the point Q(x, -y), the mirror image of P in the real axis (the x-axis). Reflection across the real axis does not change the distance from the origin, so |z| = |z-bar| = sqrt(x^2 + y^2).
markPoints: z plots at (x, y) and z-bar at (x, -y)
z-bar is the mirror image of z in the real axis
Reflection preserves distance to the origin, so |z| = |z-bar|

items.srcLabel: Authored practice (Opus) - maths-ch04 Sec 4.5 Conjugate as mirror image (Fig 4.3)
